channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. conducts another strike against Iran
item.description: U.S. forces carried out new defensive strikes on Iran on Wednesday as President Trump insisted November's midterm elections won't make him rush to make a deal to end the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: U.S. forces carried out new defensive strikes on Iran on Wednesday as President Trump insisted November's midterm elections won't make him rush to make a deal to end the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Former U.S. special envoy on the mixed messages coming from Trump and Iran
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Robert Malley, former U.S. special envoy for Iran, about mixed messages from the Trump administration and Iranian leadership about a potential deal to end the war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Robert Malley, former U.S. special envoy for Iran, about mixed messages from the Trump administration and Iranian leadership about a potential deal to end the war.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: U.S. street drug deaths keep dropping, but some Western states see deadly overdose surge
item.description: Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose reversing medication.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose reversing medication.

(Image credit: John Raby/AP)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. conducts another strike against Iran, Democrats are hopeful about their chances to retake GOP-held Senate seats, a town in eastern DRC is emerging as the epicenter of the latest Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded: U.S. conducts another strike against Iran, Democrats are hopeful about their chances to retake GOP-held Senate seats, a town in eastern DRC is emerging as the epicenter of the latest Ebola outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'Room to Move' follows a choreographer's creative journey after an autism diagnosis
item.description: The documentary "Room to Move" follows choreographer and performer Jenn Freeman as she creates her first evening-length solo performance after an autism diagnosis at age 33.
item.content:encoded: The documentary "Room to Move" follows choreographer and performer Jenn Freeman as she creates her first evening-length solo performance after an autism diagnosis at age 33.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump's proposed gas tax holiday could lead to another big problem for drivers
item.description: President Trump has proposed suspending the federal gas tax to lower the cost at the pump. But that could cost drivers another way -- potholes.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has proposed suspending the federal gas tax to lower the cost at the pump. But that could cost drivers another way -- potholes.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: '60 Minutes' journalist's contract not renewed after public dispute with Bari Weiss
item.description: Reporter Sharyn Alfonsi is a "60 Minutes" correspondent whose contract was not renewed by CBS News. Anderson Cooper has already left. What does it mean for the storied news magazine?
item.content:encoded: Reporter Sharyn Alfonsi is a "60 Minutes" correspondent whose contract was not renewed by CBS News. Anderson Cooper has already left. What does it mean for the storied news magazine?
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: More than 500 people have died in Bangladesh measles outbreak
item.description: Bangladesh is scrambling to vaccinate more children amid a measles outbreak that has killed more than 500 people, most of them children.
item.content:encoded: Bangladesh is scrambling to vaccinate more children amid a measles outbreak that has killed more than 500 people, most of them children.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: An Ohio lawmaker wants schools to teach religion's impact on U.S. history
item.description: An Ohio lawmaker is backing the Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act, which would permit teaching religion's impact on U.S. history. Opponents say it's a skewed view of history that could be divisive.
item.content:encoded: An Ohio lawmaker is backing the Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act, which would permit teaching religion's impact on U.S. history. Opponents say it's a skewed view of history that could be divisive.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Donaldson

item.title: After Texas Senate primaries, Democrats are fired up about their chances in the fall
item.description: Democrats see an opportunity to flip several GOP-held Senate seats this fall, including Texas, where the matchup was officially set this week.
item.content:encoded: Democrats see an opportunity to flip several GOP-held Senate seats this fall, including Texas, where the matchup was officially set this week.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
